titel:
Runestones of Södermanland 119

(No further context was provided, so this is a literal translation.)
html:
Standing stone made of reddish granite, approximately 1.3 meters high, about 0.5-0.8 meters wide, and about 0.5 meters thick. The height of the runes is 11-16 cm. The inscribed side faces southeast. There is no surrounding enclosure. The stone leans towards the northwest. This description has not been quality checked. Information may be missing, inaccurate, or outdated. See also the inventory book.

Location: Sweden, Södermanland County, Eskilstuna Municipality, Vallby.

Rundata identification number: Sö 119.

How certain are we about this?

This is the National Heritage Board's archaeological registration of the stone itself – a physical and documented historical monument. The runic inscription itself is a contemporary primary source, but the interpretation of the text and its context may be uncertain, and dating relies on the type and style of runes rather than specific dates.
tldr: This is a description of an old runestone located in Vallby, near Eskilstuna. It details the appearance of the stone and its location.
lattlast:
The text describes a rune stone made of reddish granite, located in Vallby, Södermanland. The stone is approximately one meter and thirty centimeters high and has an inscription on the side facing south.

The information comes from the Swedish National Heritage Board, which is the authority in Sweden responsible for protecting our historical heritage. They have documented the physical appearance of the stone but emphasize that interpreting the actual meaning of the runes can be difficult to determine with certainty.

Since there is no exact date on the stone, its creation date must be estimated by examining the style and type of runes used. It is an important historical source, even though some details of the interpretation may vary over time.
